# ============================================
# CI - Build and Test Pipeline
# ============================================
# PURPOSE: Runs on every push and PR to master
# Builds the code and runs all unit tests
# If tests fail - we know immediately before deploying
# This is the "safety net" pipeline
# ============================================

name: CI - Build and Test   # Shows in GitHub Actions tab

# TRIGGERS - when does this pipeline run?
on:
  push:                     # Run when code is pushed
    branches:
      - master              # Only on master branch
  pull_request:             # Also run on Pull Requests
    branches:
      - master              # Only PRs targeting master

# JOBS - what work gets done
jobs:
  build-and-test:           # Job name
    runs-on: windows-latest # Use Windows build server
                            # Matches our local dev environment

    steps:
      # Step 1 - Download code from GitHub onto build server
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # Step 2 - Install .NET 9 SDK on build server
      - name: Setup .NET
        uses: actions/setup-dotnet@v4
        with:
          dotnet-version: '9.0.x'   # x = latest patch version

      # Step 3 - Download all NuGet packages
      # (EF Core, Dapper, Swashbuckle, xUnit, Moq etc)
      - name: Restore dependencies
        run: dotnet restore

      # Step 4 - Compile the solution in Release mode
      # --no-restore = skip restore since we just did it
      # Release mode = optimized, no debug info
      - name: Build
        run: dotnet build --no-restore --configuration Release

      # Step 5 - Run all unit tests
      # --no-build = skip build since we just did it
      # If ANY test fails, pipeline stops here with error
      # This prevents broken code from ever deploying!
      - name: Test
        run: dotnet test --no-build --configuration Release --verbosity normal
